term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to fret, be sad, be wroth, be vexed, be enraged, be out of humour
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to be out of humour
1a2) to be enraged, be angry
2) (Qal)
2a) to appear perplexed, appear troubled
2b) to be sad-looking

verse_records.verse_text: So Abraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a skin of water and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.
When the water in the skin was gone, she put the child under one of the bushes.
Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. And she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.
When Joseph came to them in the morning, he saw that they were troubled.

Then Uzziah was angry. Now he had a censer in his hand to burn incense, and when he became angry with the priests, leprosy broke out on his forehead in the presence of the priests in the house of the Lord, by the altar of incense.
When a man's folly brings his way to ruin, his heart rages against the Lord.

and the chief of the eunuchs said to Daniel, "I fear my lord the king, who assigned your food and your drink; for why should he see that you were in worse condition than the youths who are of your own age? So you would endanger my head with the king."
Then Asa was angry with the seer and put him in the stocks in prison, for he was in a rage with him because of this. And Asa inflicted cruelties upon some of the people at the same time.

But a prophet of the Lord was there, whose name was Oded, and he went out to meet the army that came to Samaria and said to them, "Behold, because the Lord, the God of your fathers, was angry with Judah, he gave them into your hand, but you have killed them in a rage that has reached up to heaven.
A king's wrath is like the growling of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.
And the Lord will cause his majestic voice to be heard and the descending blow of his arm to be seen, in furious anger and a flame of devouring fire, with a cloudburst and storm and hailstones.
So they picked up Jonah and hurled him into the sea, and the sea ceased from its raging.
I will bear the indignation of the Lord because I have sinned against him, until he pleads my cause and executes judgment for me. He will bring me out to the light; I shall look upon his vindication.
Whatever is decreed by the God of heaven, let it be done in full for the house of the God of heaven, lest his wrath be against the realm of the king and his sons.

cross_registry_links.note: za.aph (H2196/H2197) listed in both wrath and anger word sets; related words cluster in za.aph points directly to terms in anger word set.
qe.tsaph (H7109) is the noun form of the root shared with qa.tsaph (H7107), which is an XREF term owned by registry 004. Related words cluster links the two directly.
che.met (waterskin/bottle) appears in the wrath word set; its related words cluster points to cho.mah (wall), not anger terms — connection is positional/contextual in STEP rather than semantic.
be indignant (za.am H2194) is XREF to registry 001 abomination; indignation (za.am H2195) is XREF to registry 004 anger — both appear in the wrath STEP term list.
excitement (ir H5892A) is XREF to registry 005 anguish — its presence in the wrath word set indicates overlap between arousal/agitation states.
vexation (ka.as H3708A) is XREF to registry 071 grief — vexation bridges anger and grief domains.

data_quality_flags.description: MTI provisional number H2195A (assigned at Session Startup before STEP extraction) corrected to two authoritative Strong's numbers: H2196 (za.aph verb, 'to enrage') and H2197 (za.aph noun, 'rage'). The source file reveals za.aph is a dual-entry term with distinct verb and noun Strong's. Both recorded as separate term records.

MTI provisional number H7110C corrected to H7109 (qe.tsaph noun, 'wrath of God'). The source file block for qe.tsaph also contains H7107 (qa.tsaph, 'be angry') but H7107 is an existing XREF term owned by registry 004 anger and is not extracted here.

che.met (bottle/waterskin) occurs approximately 3 times. All 3 verses recorded. Term is a concrete object noun (waterskin) present in the wrath word set likely as a somatic/metonymic container for the emotion concept. Meaning field is sparse.
qe.tsaph (wrath noun H7109) occurs approximately 1 time. Single verse recorded (Ezr 7:23). Very sparse data; this noun form is the rarest of the qe.tsaph root family entries.
za.aph verb form (H2196) occurs approximately 5 times. 4 verses recorded (80%). Acceptable but limited.
